message:
chore(cargo): replace 'asupersync-runtime is no-op' comment with accurate description (ft-f6vhg)

ft-f6vhg's premise was "asupersync-runtime is a no-op feature flag —
delete it, drop every required-features reference, ship." Verification
revealed the premise was wrong: the feature gates a substantial test
surface that prevents safe deletion under the SLA.

What the verification found:

  * 50 test files use `#![cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` at the
    top of the file to opt into asupersync-specific Cx-aware APIs
    (sleep_with_cx, reserve(&cx), timeout_with_cx, etc.).
  * 10 bench files do the same.
  * 16 [[bench]] / [[test]] declarations carry
    `required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]`.
  * Multiple files (runtime_compat_tests.rs, robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs,
    proptest_runtime_compat.rs, replay_labruntime.rs, web.rs,
    mux_pool_scaling.rs, tailer.rs) carry PAIRED
    `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` /
    `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` bodies. The
    not(...) body uses the legacy tokio-shape API (`reserve()` with
    no Cx, etc.) — it would fail to compile against today's
    asupersync runtime if the feature gate were removed.

Concrete repro: trial-deleting the feature definition at line 677 +
its default-features entry + every `required-features` reference,
then running `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets`,
produced compile errors:

  * `tailer` and `sync_primitives` benches failed with
    `error[E0601]: main function not found in crate` — file-level
    gates evaluated to false, dropping the entire bench module.
  * `tailer` body's no-feature `tx.reserve()` broke with
    `error[E0061]: this method takes 1 argument but 0 arguments
    were supplied` — the asupersync runtime requires `reserve(&cx)`,
    which only the WITH-feature body uses.

The right cleanup sequence is:

  1. Audit each `cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))` body and
     decide whether to delete (it's dead-code from the pre-asupersync
     era) or to convert to a feature-independent shape.
  2. After every paired `not(...)` body is gone, drop the file-level
     `#![cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` gates.
  3. Then it is safe to delete the feature definition + the
     required-features references + the default-features entry.

That sequence is too large to land in this bead's SLA. Filed as
ft-nm5nc (parent-child of ft-f6vhg) with the full retirement plan +
inputs (rg patterns + file lists).

This commit only updates the Cargo.toml comment that incorrectly
described the feature as a "no-op." The corrected text describes
its real role — test/bench selector preserving paired legacy-tokio
probes for the migration-conformance suite — and points at the
follow-up bead.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets

Build clean. No production behavior change.

files.patch:
@@ -674,7 +674,18 @@ distributed = [
     "dep:x509-parser",
 ]
 sync = []
-asupersync-runtime = []  # no-op: asupersync is now always-on (ft-xbnl0.2.5)
+# Test/bench selector: gates ~50 labruntime tests and ~10 benches that
+# require asupersync-specific Cx-aware APIs (sleep_with_cx, reserve(&cx),
+# etc.). Asupersync IS the sole async runtime in production code paths
+# (ft-xbnl0.2.5), so the feature does NOT change which production code
+# compiles — but several test/bench files use #![cfg(feature =
+# "asupersync-runtime")] to opt into the asupersync-specific paths and
+# others use #[cfg(not(...))] to keep legacy tokio-shape probes alive
+# for the migration-conformance suite. ft-f6vhg verified that simply
+# deleting the feature breaks ~50 test files; full retirement requires
+# untangling the paired cfg(not(...)) bodies first. Don't call this a
+# no-op until those are gone.
+asupersync-runtime = []
 recorder-lexical = ["dep:tantivy"]
 frankensqlite-recorder = []
 disk-pressure = ["dep:nix"]
